text:
Citygate Neurology Clinic

Department of Clinical Neurosciences

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025

Dr Emily Carter
Citygate Health Centre
12 Albion Street, Manchester M1 4AB

Dear Dr Emily Carter,

William Harris, DOB: 15-02-1965, Hospital No: H563829 NHS No. 9273645810
10 Meadow Park, London, EC1V 9BT

Summary/Diagnoses: Focal epilepsy with sensory-motor onset, previous traumatic brain injury, progressive cognitive slowing and executive dysfunction, osteoporosis, past alcohol dependence (currently abstinent), lives in supported accommodation, active smoker.

Medication: Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily; Carbamazepine MR 400 mg twice daily; Topiramate 75 mg morning and 100 mg evening; Thiamine; Calcium/Vitamin D; Atorvastatin; Lansoprazole; Bisoprolol; Ramipril; Carbocisteine; Emollients.

I reviewed Bill in clinic today, accompanied by his key worker who has supported him for the last year. He reports increasing sensory intolerance to brightly lit, acoustically harsh environments. Over the last six months he describes more rapid-onset events with left facial pulling followed by jerking of the left arm, preserved awareness, then brief speech arrest. These episodes now arise chiefly during shifts near stage lighting rigs at his day centre and on the factory floor during machinery testing, with faster evolution than previously described.

Seizures: He remains abstinent from alcohol; however, he and staff note a pattern of attacks clustering after evenings spent in loud, high-glare venues at the centre, consistent with episodes that tend to follow exposure rather than intake. Frequency has increased: July x 3 focal aware motor; August x 4 focal aware motor (two progressing to impaired awareness); September x 5 focal aware motor with two focal to bilateral tonic–clonic. This represents a clear deterioration compared with earlier months when events were sporadic and slower in onset. A seizure diary kept by staff corroborates this trend, particularly on days involving rigging under bright LED arrays and during compressor testing.

Neurological examination: Alert and oriented; speech mildly hesitant. Subtle left nasolabial flattening at rest. Increased tone right lower limb with circumduction on gait testing; Romberg negative. No new cranial nerve deficits. Fundoscopy unremarkable. Cognitive screen shows slowed processing speed compared with prior review.

Epilepsy classification: The clinical semiology, consistent lateralising signs, and evolution support a diagnosis of focal epilepsy. There is no evidence of a primary generalised epilepsy syndrome.

Risk/precipitating factors: Photopic glare and complex acoustic environments appear to act as triggers. Sleep is fragmented on nights after late activities at the centre. No current alcohol use.

Plan:
- Acknowledge deterioration with increased event rate and faster evolution in bright, high-noise settings.
- Arrange urgent EEG with photic stimulation and patterned stimulus, plus prolonged ambulatory recording across a typical day at the centre if feasible.
- MRI brain (epilepsy protocol) to reassess focal cortical injury sequelae post-TBI.
- No immediate change to antiepileptic regimen today pending investigations; consider levetiracetam uptitration versus switch to lacosamide at next review, guided by EEG and tolerability.
- Provide written guidance to day centre to minimise triggers: reduce exposure to intense LED arrays, allow shaded rest areas, use hearing protection during machinery testing, scheduled breaks to reduce sensory load.
- Reinforce seizure diary with precise logging of lighting/sound exposure and sleep the preceding night.
- Check trough carbamazepine level, U&E, LFT, FBC, vitamin D.
- Bone health: continue Calcium/Vitamin D; DEXA due next year.

Follow-up: Review in 8 weeks, sooner if further focal to bilateral tonic–clonic events occur.

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: September x 5 focal aware motor with two focal to bilateral tonic–clonic.

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit numeric seizure counts: July = 3, August = 4, September = 5. The clinic date is 02 Oct 2025, so the latest reported month is September with 5 events. Per instruction to select the highest frequency when multiple frequencies are given, choose 5 per month. Cluster mention lacks numeric detail, so monthly counts take priority.
seizure_frequency_number: September x 5 focal aware motor with two focal to bilateral tonic–clonic.

flaws.description: Gold (5 per month) disagrees with Reference (unknown)
